items.title: Add mandatory self-hosted ROCm runtime workflow (gfx90a)
acceptance_criteria: PRs run a self-hosted ROCm runtime job on labeled gfx90a runners
Runtime job fails when GPU runtime prerequisites are missing
hipStateVec and hipTensorNet runtime tests execute on real ROCm hardware
items.notes: Self-hosted gfx90a runtime workflow is source-defined and contract-tested; real pass/fail artifacts still require an attached ROCm runner.
items.verification_path: Run tests/test_rocm_compatibility_contract.py locally; run '.github/workflows/rocm-ci.yml' job 'rocm-runtime-self-hosted' on a labeled gfx90a runner and attach artifacts.

items.title: Add ROCm environment probe script and enforce fail-fast
acceptance_criteria: Probe checks hipcc, rocminfo, rocm-smi and /dev/kfd
Runtime lanes fail early with actionable diagnostics
tests: Unit shell check of probe script
items.notes: Reusable ROCm runtime probe now checks hipcc, rocminfo, rocm-smi, and /dev/kfd before configure/build in self-hosted runtime lanes. Actual artifact capture still requires a ROCm runner.
items.verification_path: Run tests/test_rocm_compatibility_contract.py locally; run '.github/workflows/rocm-ci.yml' and verify rocm-runtime-probe.log plus /dev/kfd fail-fast behavior on a ROCm runner.

items.title: Publish explicit ROCm support policy (min ROCm + arch lanes)
acceptance_criteria: Policy specifies min ROCm 6.2.2
Policy defines primary gfx90a and legacy gfx906/gfx908 lanes
Policy is referenced from CI docs
items.notes: Current defaults and CI lanes do not encode architecture policy.
items.verification_path: Policy file is present; confirm CI/docs references remain current in PR review.

items.title: Implement non-local distributed fallback for single- and two-qubit gates
acceptance_criteria: Distributed H/X/Y/Z/CNOT/CZ paths no longer fail on non-local qubits for small circuits
Fallback uses explicit swap orchestration and restores qubit layout
tests: Gate parity tests for non-local target/control combinations
items.notes: Non-local named single-qubit and controlled single-qubit/CNOT/CZ paths now localize touched qubits with rocsvSwapIndexBits, apply the local kernel, and restore the original layout; broader generic matrix cases still rely on the explicit host fallback. Performance optimization remains separate.
items.verification_path: Run rocQuantum-main/tests/test_statevec_fastpath_contract.py locally; run 2-rank non-local H/X/Y/Z/CNOT/CZ parity tests on a ROCm multi-GPU runner for hardware evidence.

items.title: Add distributed sampling and expectation host fallback
acceptance_criteria: Distributed sampling returns valid shot outputs
Distributed expectation APIs return finite values on smoke circuits
Fallback mode is clearly marked as slow
tests: Expectation parity checks (distributed vs single device)
items.notes: Distributed expectation and shot sampling now route through RCCL first, then an explicit opt-in host fallback that gathers the distributed state and computes host Pauli expectations or host shot samples. This is correctness/debug only; runtime parity still needs ROCm multi-GPU artifacts.
items.verification_path: Run rocQuantum-main/tests/test_rccl_distributed_contract.py locally; run distributed expectation and rocsvSample host-fallback smoke tests on a ROCm multi-GPU runner for hardware evidence.

items.title: Replace inconsistent multi-GPU guide with behavior-accurate matrix
acceptance_criteria: Guide contains one consistent status section
Claims about RCCL/allreduce match actual code paths
Known limitations table covers all distributed NOT_IMPLEMENTED APIs
tests: Link from README or developer docs
items.notes: Guide now has a single behavior matrix, runtime switch table, and limitations table aligned with source-contract tests. Runtime validation remains tracked by separate ROCm CI items.
items.verification_path: Run tests/test_statevec_fastpath_contract.py, tests/test_rccl_distributed_contract.py, and tests/test_rocm_compatibility_contract.py; runtime behavior still requires ROCm self-hosted CI artifacts.

items.title: Introduce distributed communication abstraction with explicit fallback mode
acceptance_criteria: Swap/remap path calls a comm abstraction layer
Runtime exposes active backend (host_fallback or rccl)
Fallback is documented as non-performance path
tests: Integration test with forced host fallback
items.notes: Runtime now exposes distributed backend introspection (`none`, `host_fallback`, `rccl`) while keeping existing explicit host fallback and optional RCCL paths unchanged. Real multi-GPU behavior still needs ROCm runner evidence.
items.verification_path: Run rocQuantum-main/tests/test_rccl_distributed_contract.py locally; run distributed backend introspection plus host/RCCL A/B smoke tests on a ROCm multi-GPU runner.

items.title: Gate skeleton providers behind explicit experimental opt-in
acceptance_criteria: set_target() blocks skeleton providers by default
Opt-in switch is documented and tested
tests: Unit tests for list_backends() and set_target() policy behavior
items.notes: Prevents user-facing exposure of placeholder backends as stable features.
items.verification_path: Run tests/test_provider_backend_contract.py plus the full Python test suite; provider credential lanes remain required for real non-skeleton backends.

items.title: Complete distributed controlled matrix and multi-control non-local paths
acceptance_criteria: Controlled matrix supports non-local controls/targets for tested arities
Multi-control/multi-target distributed paths avoid NOT_IMPLEMENTED in common cases
tests: Stress tests for control-target locality permutations
items.notes: Common non-local distributed multi-control paths now use swap-localization: controlled single-qubit/controlled-matrix 1x1, MCX, and CSWAP localize touched qubits, run existing local kernels, and restore layout. Broader high-arity generic controlled matrices remain bounded by explicit host fallback or future device kernels.
items.verification_path: Run rocQuantum-main/tests/test_statevec_fastpath_contract.py locally; run 2-rank non-local controlled-matrix, MCX, and CSWAP parity tests on a ROCm multi-GPU runner.

items.title: Implement RCCL communication backend for distributed remap/reductions
acceptance_criteria: RCCL backend executes swap/remap without host gather path
Measurement/reduction paths use device collectives where applicable
tests: A/B parity tests host fallback vs RCCL mode
items.notes: RCCL communicators now cover supported local-domain reductions with ncclAllReduce and non-local distributed swap/remap with ncclSend/ncclRecv for rank/rank plus local/rank swaps. Host gather remap is no longer the default; it requires explicit host-fallback mode when RCCL is unavailable. Real A/B parity and timing artifacts still require a ROCm multi-GPU runner.
items.verification_path: Run rocQuantum-main/tests/test_rccl_distributed_contract.py locally; run 2-rank rank/rank and local/rank rocsvSwapIndexBits parity tests plus expectation/sample reduction tests on a ROCm multi-GPU runner and attach artifacts.

items.title: Wire optimizer algorithm selection through runtime contraction path
acceptance_criteria: pathfinder_algorithm influences contraction order selection
Unavailable algorithms fall back to greedy with warnings
tests: Contraction regression under greedy and fallback paths
items.notes: Runtime contraction planning now uses an effective pathfinder algorithm: unavailable METIS/KAHYPAR requests fall back to GREEDY, and the Python binding emits an explicit warning that points callers to get_tensornet_capabilities(). Real METIS/KAHYPAR library integration remains P1-TN-002.
items.verification_path: Run rocQuantum-main/tests/test_tensornet_contract.py locally; run TensorNet contraction fallback smoke tests on a ROCm runner for hardware evidence.

items.title: Add optional METIS path integration with build guard
acceptance_criteria: Build option toggles METIS support
Selecting METIS without support falls back cleanly
items.notes: TensorNet now has an explicit ROCQUANTUM_TENSORNET_ENABLE_METIS build option. When enabled, CMake requires METIS headers/library before defining HAS_METIS; runtime contraction planning calls METIS_PartGraphKway for partition-biased pair selection. When METIS support is absent, requested METIS continues to fall back cleanly to GREEDY with Python warnings and capability metadata.
items.verification_path: Run rocQuantum-main/tests/test_tensornet_contract.py locally; build TensorNet with ROCQUANTUM_TENSORNET_ENABLE_METIS=OFF and ON against a METIS install, then run TensorNet contraction regression on a ROCm runner.

items.title: Expand dtype support from C64 baseline to include C128 lane
acceptance_criteria: rocTensorNetworkCreate accepts C128 when build supports it
Docs and tests declare exact supported dtype matrix
tests: Contraction correctness regression for each supported dtype
items.notes: C64 remains the default build lane; C128 is accepted in ROCQ_PRECISION_DOUBLE builds and now dispatches TensorNet GEMM/SVD through double-complex rocBLAS/rocSOLVER routines. C32 and mixed precision remain P2.
items.verification_path: Run rocQuantum-main/tests/test_tensornet_contract.py locally; run TensorNet contraction/SVD dtype smoke tests for C64 and ROCQ_PRECISION_DOUBLE C128 builds on a ROCm runner.

items.title: Implement compile_and_execute MVP for MLIR gate replay
acceptance_criteria: compile_and_execute executes supported MLIR gate subset
returns final statevector
binding forwards runtime args and diagnostics
tests: ROCm runtime tests for Bell/parametric circuits
items.notes: MVP Python and source-contract path is implemented; real ROCm runtime validation is still required for hardware evidence.
items.verification_path: Run tests/test_p1_compiler.py, tests/test_runtime_api_contract.py, and tests/test_e2e_compiler_python_flows.py locally; run the same compile_and_execute path on a ROCm host (/dev/kfd) for hardware evidence.

items.title: Add backend capability listing API with status metadata
acceptance_criteria: API returns backend status labels (implemented/partial/skeleton)
CLI/docs expose capability inspection before target selection
items.notes: Backend status metadata is exposed through list_backends() and rocq list-backends; skeleton providers remain opt-in only.
items.verification_path: Run tests/test_provider_backend_contract.py and tests/test_p0_fixes.py; provider credential lanes remain required for real non-skeleton backends.

items.title: Replace host fallback reductions with device collectives and optimize scale
acceptance_criteria: No host gather/scatter in performance-critical distributed operations
Measured speedup over host fallback baseline
items.notes: P1 RCCL backend is now present. Pauli expectation/sample reductions, local-domain dense matrix expectation, and rank-local distributed CSR sparse moments avoid full host state gather by reducing per-rank device results through ncclAllReduce; sparse moments still host-stage CSR slicing before per-rank uploads. Non-local 1-4 target generic dense matrix apply now uses swap-localization before the explicit host fallback, distributed_reduction_benchmark emits dense_expectation_ms, sparse_moments_ms, and generic_matrix_ms for RCCL-vs-host artifacts, and the release benchmark runner summarizes host-fallback-over-RCCL speedup ratios when both cases run. Broad non-local sparse distributed reductions and measured speedups still need ROCm multi-GPU CI artifacts.
items.verification_path: Run rocQuantum-main/tests/test_rccl_distributed_contract.py and rocQuantum-main/tests/test_benchmark_release_contract.py locally; run distributed Pauli, sample/probabilities, local-domain dense matrix expectation, rank-local sparse CSR moments, generic dense matrix apply parity, and distributed_reduction_benchmark on a ROCm multi-GPU runner.

items.title: Advance optimizer parity (slicing, advanced heuristics, mixed precision)
acceptance_criteria: Slicing policy tied to memory limits with reproducible behavior
Mixed precision plan documented and tested
items.notes: TensorNet pair contractions now pass memory_limit_bytes/num_slices into rocTensorContractPair_internal, which deterministically splits the contracted GEMM K dimension and accumulates slices. Capability metadata reports this limited runtime slicing support. The mixed-precision lane remains explicitly documented as build-scoped C64/C128 today with future conversion/accumulation policy required before simultaneous runtime mixed precision.
items.verification_path: Run rocQuantum-main/tests/test_tensornet_contract.py locally; run TensorNet contraction regression plus SlicingLogicTest in C64 and ROCQ_PRECISION_DOUBLE C128 builds on a ROCm runner for numerical stability evidence.
